featured.name: Deep Agents
featured.tagline: Structured runtime for planning, memory, and context isolation in multi-step AI agents.
featured.why_radar: LangChain's answer to the multi-step agent problem. Most agents break down when tasks get stateful and complex. Deep Agents tackles this with proper isolation and memory management, something the ecosystem desperately needs.

featured.name: GLM-OCR
featured.tagline: 0.9B multimodal OCR model for document parsing and key information extraction.
featured.why_radar: Zhipu AI's compact OCR model that handles real documents, not demo screenshots. At 0.9B parameters, it's actually usable for production workflows without melting your infrastructure. The focus on structured extraction makes this practical for business use.

picks.name: gstack
picks.tagline: Open-source Claude Code system for planning, code review, QA, and shipping.
picks.why_radar: Garry Tan's opinionated take on AI-assisted development workflows. Eight distinct skills that separate planning from execution. It's Claude Code packaged into something resembling a proper engineering process.

picks.name: Aletheia
picks.tagline: AI agent for autonomous professional research discoveries beyond math competitions.
picks.why_radar: Google DeepMind's attempt to move from Olympic-level math problems to actual research. The jump from competition formats to open-ended discovery is massive. If they've cracked iterative reasoning for real research, this changes everything.

picks.name: GitAgent
picks.tagline: Open standard that turns any Git repo into an AI agent.
picks.why_radar: An interesting approach to making codebases AI-readable. Instead of cramming everything into context windows, GitAgent creates a standard for repos to describe themselves. Could solve the 'AI doesn't understand my project structure' problem.

picks.name: KeyID
picks.tagline: Free email and phone infrastructure for AI agents with MCP support.
picks.why_radar: AI agents need real communication channels, not just API calls. KeyID provides the boring infrastructure that makes agent-to-human communication possible. The MCP integration shows they understand the ecosystem.

hn_top5.title: Show HN: OpenClaw-superpowers – Self-modifying skill library for OpenClaw agents
hn_top5.summary: Someone's built a self-modifying skill library for OpenClaw agents that apparently gives them 'superpowers'. The GitHub link suggests it's another attempt to make AI agents more capable through dynamic skill acquisition. Zero comments indicates the community isn't particularly excited about yet another AI agent framework.

hn_top5.title: A persistent AI I built to stop LLMs from resetting
hn_top5.summary: A developer created persistent AI to solve the annoying problem of LLMs forgetting everything between conversations. This addresses a real pain point that anyone using ChatGPT or similar tools has experienced. The low engagement suggests either the solution isn't particularly novel or the post lacks detail.

hn_top5.title: Ask HN: How do you prompt AI to talk to you?
hn_top5.summary: Someone's asking the community for tips on how to prompt AI effectively for conversation. This is a basic question that suggests the poster is new to working with language models. The minimal engagement reflects that most HN users have moved beyond beginner prompting questions.

hn_top5.title: You Need Deterministic Gates for Probabilistic AI Agents
hn_top5.summary: An article arguing that AI agents need deterministic control mechanisms despite their probabilistic nature. The core idea makes sense since you want reliable behaviour in production systems, not random outputs. The contradictory URL title suggests some confusion about the actual argument being made.

hn_top5.title: Show HN: Lfg – WoW raid frames for AI agents on a $25 LED
hn_top5.summary: A bizarre project that displays AI agent status using World of Warcraft-style raid frames on a cheap LED display. It's a creative way to monitor multiple AI agents simultaneously, borrowing familiar UI patterns from gaming. The novelty factor is high but practical applications seem limited.
